title: salix thurberi
intro: Salix thurberi, also known as Thurber's willow, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Salix thurberi include reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating menstrual cramps, among other uses. Herbal preparations of Salix thurberi include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
